vision_report.maker_checker_mechanism: As the Garden Botanical Observer, I first performed a 'Maker' phase, which involved an initial comprehensive scan of all provided images to establish a baseline understanding of the scene, identify all visible containers, and mentally map them against the 'Expected Biome Registry'. This initial scan helped me formulate a systematic approach for the chronological audit, noting potential points of interest, missing elements (like the scale anchor), and recurring objects. Following this, I entered the 'Checker' phase, where I meticulously audited each image chronologically from 'EARLIEST' to 'CURRENT'. In this phase, I described the contents of each image in detail, focusing on plant health, soil conditions, and any environmental changes. After describing each image (from T-5 onwards), I explicitly stated 'what changed' compared to the preceding image, ensuring a precise temporal delta analysis. I then validated my observations against the registry, identified any anomalies or systemic losses, and synthesized all findings into the required JSON structure, ensuring all specified keys are populated based *solely* on visual evidence.

pots_identified.description: Yellow/gold colored pot, visible in EARLIEST and T-5. Contains soil and dried debris.
pots_identified.registry_status: Not in registry (New Introduction/Intervention as a container)

pots_identified.description: Black colored pot, visible in all relevant images. Contains soil, eggshell fragments, and from T-3 onwards, a plant.
White plastic cup, visible in T-1 and CURRENT. Contains clear liquid and a green leaf/reflection.
pots_identified.registry_status: Not in registry (New Introduction/Intervention as a container)

missing_registry_items.reason: No pot explicitly identified as P3, nor any plant associated with it.

P2_Mexican_Mint_Black_Pot.details: A plant is observed in the black pot (Pot B) from T-3 onwards, consistent with the registry. It appears healthy and vigorous.

P3_White_Rabbit_Anchor.details: Neither the P3 pot nor the white rabbit scale anchor is visible in any of the provided images.

Pot_A_Yellow_Gold.EARLIEST: No plant visible. Contains dark, loose soil with some dried plant matter/debris.
Pot_A_Yellow_Gold.T-5: No plant visible. Contents identical to EARLIEST.

Pot_B_Black.EARLIEST: Bare, dark, moist-looking soil with scattered small, white, irregular fragments (consistent with crushed eggshells). No plant visible.
Pot_B_Black.T-5: Bare, dark, moist-looking soil with scattered white fragments (eggshells). No plant visible. Identical to EARLIEST.
Pot_B_Black.T-3: A single, young plant with approximately 4-5 rounded, turgid, healthy green leaves is present. Soil is dark and moist, with eggshell fragments still visible.
Pot_B_Black.T-2: Plant appears largely the same as T-3, with healthy, turgid green leaves. Soil remains moist with eggshells.
Pot_B_Black.T-1: Plant continues to show healthy, turgid green leaves. No significant changes in leaf count or color. Soil appears consistently moist, eggshell fragments visible.
Pot_B_Black.CURRENT: Plant maintains its healthy appearance, with turgid, rounded green leaves. No signs of wilting, discoloration, or leaf loss. Soil surface remains dark and moist, with eggshell fragments.

Container_C_White_Cup.T-1: A new white plastic cup is present, containing clear liquid. A reflection of a green leaf or a small plant specimen is visible within the liquid.
Container_C_White_Cup.CURRENT: The white cup is still present with liquid. The reflection or plant specimen within the liquid appears consistent with T-1.

soil_texture.Pot_B: Consistently dark and moist from EARLIEST to CURRENT. No cracking or desiccation observed.

biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds, moss, secondary seedlings, or uncatalogued sprouts observed in any pot.
biome_observations.debris_on_desk: Wires (yellow, red, white), electronic components (circuit boards), a blue object (book/box), a white pen, and a white cylindrical object (EARLIEST, T-5) are present. Eggshell fragments are consistently present on the soil surface of Pot B.

temporal_deltas.changes: Minimal to no discernible changes in pot contents or arrangement. The scene appears largely static.
Complete loss of visual data; the image is entirely black. This represents a systemic data acquisition anomaly for this time point.

Significant compositional change. A young plant has been introduced into Pot B (black pot). Pot A (yellow/gold) and the white cylindrical object are no longer in the frame. New electronic components (circuit board with wires), a blue object, and a white pen are now visible on the desk surface.
Slight shift in camera angle/framing, revealing more of the soil surface in Pot B. The plant's appearance remains stable. Electronic components and other desk items are consistent.

Introduction of a new container, Container C (white cup), to the right of Pot B. It contains liquid and a green leaf/reflection. A small purple circuit board is now more clearly visible near the top edge of Pot B.
No significant changes observed in the plant in Pot B or the contents of Container C. The overall scene remains stable.

visual_health_inference.plant_in_Pot_B: The plant in Pot B (black pot) is visually healthy. Its leaves are consistently green, turgid, and show no signs of wilting, discoloration, necrosis, or pest damage throughout its observed presence (T-3 to CURRENT). The soil appears adequately moist, supporting healthy growth.
visual_health_inference.plant_in_Container_C: The green leaf/reflection in Container C (white cup) appears stable and healthy, submerged/floating in clear liquid, with no visible signs of decay or stress.

anomalies.details: The expected scale anchor is not present in any image.
No pot identified as P3 is visible.
The image for T-4 is entirely black, indicating a complete loss of visual data for that timestamp.
This pot is present in earlier images but not listed in the registry.
This container is introduced in T-1 and is not part of the initial registry.
The consistent presence of eggshell fragments suggests an intentional soil amendment or pest deterrent, not explicitly mentioned in the registry.
Multiple circuit boards and wires are consistently present, indicating a monitoring or experimental setup that is beyond typical plant care.

vision_report.narrative_description: The observation period reveals a dynamic indoor botanical setup over several days. Initially, two pots were visible: a yellow/gold pot (Pot A) with dry soil and debris, and a black pot (Pot B) containing moist soil and scattered eggshell fragments. A significant event occurred between T-4 (a data loss) and T-3, marked by the introduction of a healthy, young plant with 4-5 rounded green leaves into Pot B. This plant, consistent with the registry's 'Mexican Mint' for P2, has maintained excellent turgor and vibrant green color throughout the subsequent images, indicating robust health. Later in the sequence, a white cup (Container C) containing liquid and a green leaf/reflection was introduced. The soil in Pot B consistently appears moist, and no incidental growth or fungal issues are observed. Electronic components are a persistent feature, suggesting an ongoing experimental or monitoring setup. A notable 'Systemic Loss' is the complete absence of the expected 'P3' pot and its 'white rabbit' scale anchor from all observations. Overall, the primary plant specimen in Pot B is thriving, and the biome appears well-maintained, albeit with several uncatalogued additions and a data anomaly.
